name: SwarmOrchestrator
description: Python orchestration skill: local multi-agent workflows via blackboard file, permission gating, and token budget scripts. All execution is local — no network calls, no Node.js required. NOTE: The README describes the full project including the companion Node.js/TypeScript npm package (network-ai on npm). Features documented there — HMAC-signed tokens, AES-256 encryption, networked MCP server, 14 adapters, full CLI — are NOT implemented in these Python scripts and are NOT part of this ClawHub skill bundle. Install 'npm install -g network-ai' separately for those features.
author: Network-AI Community

install.description: No install step required. All Python scripts use standard library only (zero third-party packages). Simply ensure python3 is available and run scripts directly from the scripts/ directory.

python.requirements_note: requirements.txt contains ZERO required packages — all scripts use Python stdlib only. No pip install needed.
python.install_command: # No install needed. Optionally: pip install -r requirements.txt (installs nothing required)
python.note: All scripts run locally only. No external network calls. No third-party dependencies.

optional_node_server.description: Separate optional component — NOT part of this skill bundle. For MCP/IDE integration only.
optional_node_server.note: This npm package contains the Node.js TypeScript server. It is NOT fetched automatically by this skill. Users who want MCP integration must install it manually.

delegate_task.description: Delegate a task to a specialized sub-agent within the swarm

targetAgent.description: The identifier of the target agent to receive the task

taskPayload.description: The task context, instructions, and any required data

priority.description: Task priority level for queue ordering

requiresAuth.description: Whether this task requires AuthGuardian permission grant

query_swarm_state.description: Query the current state of the agent swarm, including active tasks, blackboard contents, and agent availability

scope.description: The scope of state information to retrieve

agentFilter.description: Optional list of agent IDs to filter results

spawn_parallel_agents.description: Spawn multiple sub-agents in parallel for complex task decomposition

tasks.description: Array of parallel tasks to execute

synthesisStrategy.description: How to combine results from parallel agents

request_permission.description: Request permission from AuthGuardian for sensitive operations

resourceType.description: The type of protected resource being requested

update_blackboard.description: Write or update entries on the shared blackboard for cross-agent coordination

inject_context.description: Read the persistent project context file and return a formatted markdown block for injection into an agent system prompt. This is Layer 3 (long-lived) memory — architecture decisions, goals, tech stack, milestones, and banned approaches that every agent in the swarm should know.

contextMarkdown.description: Formatted markdown block ready to prepend to an agent system prompt

updatedAt.description: ISO timestamp of last context update

update_context.description: Persist a decision, milestone update, stack entry, goal, or banned approach to the project context file (Layer 3 memory). Changes are appended and survive across sessions.

add.description: Item to append (JSON object or plain string, depending on section)

set.description: Key-value pairs to merge into the section (use for stack and project)

complete.description: Milestone name to mark completed (milestones section only)

adapters.note: No adapter dependencies are required at install time. Import only what you use.

config.maxParallelAgents_default: Infinity (no hard cap since v4.0.0 — set to a positive integer to enforce a limit)

SWARM_TOKEN_SECRET.description: Node.js MCP server only — NOT used by the Python scripts in this skill bundle. Used by the optional network-ai npm package (network-ai-server) as the HMAC secret for its AuthGuardian token implementation. The Python scripts use UUID-based grant tokens stored in data/active_grants.json.

SWARM_ENCRYPTION_KEY.description: Node.js MCP server only — NOT used by the Python scripts in this skill bundle. Used by the optional network-ai npm package for AES-256 blackboard encryption. The Python scripts do not encrypt blackboard data.

OPENAI_API_KEY.description: Not used by the Python scripts in this skill bundle. Only used by the optional Node.js demo examples (07-full-showcase.ts) when running the companion npm package.

audit_log.description: Append-only JSONL audit log recording operation metadata (agentId, action, timestamp, outcome). Stays in the local data/ directory. No data is sent externally. Can be disabled with --no-audit flag or audit_log: false in config.
